query_normalized: self-distilled agentic reinforcement learning computer science
rationale: The paper focuses on Inverse Reinforcement Learning (IRL) and hierarchical planning, which is a distinct mechanism from Self-Distilled Agentic Reinforcement Learning (which typically involves an agent learning from its own distilled outputs or policies). While both fall under the broad domain of Reinforcement Learning, the paper does not measure the specific mechanism of self-distillation, nor does it address the agentic loop or the specific variables (distillation, self-training) central to the
